similar_articles.context: Semantic similarity in CTA block (closing section about legal consultation)

conflicts: cta_overlap: 0.245 (threshold: 0.20) — closing section has similar semantic structure to another article's CTA

analysis.detail: The draft's closing section (final 2-3 paragraphs about legal help) has 0.245 cosine similarity with the closing section of 'cessiya-ustupka-trebovaniya-prava-dolzhnika'. This exceeds the threshold of 0.20. The similarity stems from parallel structures discussing when to engage a lawyer and what lawyers check (both articles use similar pattern: 'document exists + seems clear BUT process mistakes exist — consult specialist'). Content topics are distinct (employer damages vs. debt assignment), but semantic closing pattern is too similar.
analysis.note: Structure (H2 sequence) and thesis similarity are well below thresholds (0.0 and 0.216 respectively). Only CTA is problematic.

action_for_agent_4: Change CTA angle in the final section (lines 115-120). Instead of the template 'everything looks clear BUT process matters require expertise', use one of these angles: (1) Specific outcome focus: 'You may recover 100%, 50%, or nothing — here's what changes the result' (reframe from consultation need to outcome uncertainty); (2) Risk-first angle: 'Biggest mistake employers make when calculating damages: ...'; (3) Procedural checklist: Convert final section to a verification checklist employees must NOT skip, rather than 'get legal help' message. This will shift the semantic pattern and reduce CTA overlap.
